author: śrīmadbhaktideśika ācārya mahārāja
verses:
śrī⋅rādhikā⋅rūpa⋅guṇormi⋅cauraḥ
prataptakārtasvarakānta⋅gauraḥ
vedānta⋅vedāṅga⋅purāṇa⋅sāraḥ
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

brahmendra⋅rudrastuta⋅pāda⋅padmaḥ
audārya⋅mādhurya⋅guṇābdhisadma
romāñca⋅kampāśru⋅pramoda⋅bhāraḥ
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

svarūpa⋅rūpādika⋅prāṇa⋅nāthaḥ
gopāla⋅govinda⋅mukunda⋅nāthaḥ
daridra⋅durjātyagha⋅duḥkhadāraḥ
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

māyāmata⋅dhvānta⋅nikārahārī
vārāṇasī⋅nyāsi⋅samūha⋅tārī
viśuddha⋅sadbhakti⋅prasāra⋅kārī
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

śrī⋅digvijetṛ⋅dvija⋅darpahārī
śrī⋅sārvabhaumāti⋅prasāda⋅kārī
aṣṭādaśāvdeśa⋅purī⋅vihārī
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

mahojjvala⋅premarasa⋅pradātā
śrī⋅nāma⋅sarvottama⋅bhakti⋅dhātā
goloka⋅vṛndāvana⋅sad⋅vihāraḥ
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

sadā harekṛṣṇa⋅sugāna⋅mattaḥ
jogīndra⋅munīndra⋅samādhi⋅vittaḥ
dattavrajaprema⋅sudhā⋅susāraḥ
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

kavāṭa⋅vakṣo⋅nava⋅padma⋅netraḥ
śrī⋅saccidānanda⋅ghanāsugātraḥ
svāṅga⋅prabhā⋅nindita⋅koṭimāraḥ
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

nīlādri⋅śuvrāṁśu⋅sudhācakorāḥ
rathāgra⋅saṅgīta⋅sudhāvidhūraḥ
śrī⋅vaiṣṇava⋅vrāta⋅lasaccharīraḥ
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

bhaktāvalī⋅mānasa⋅rāja⋅haṁsaḥ
sannyāsi⋅bhūdeva⋅kulavataṁsaḥ
śrīmajjagannātha⋅śacī⋅kumāraḥ
jīyāt sa gauraḥ karuṇā⋅vatāraḥ

gaura⋅stutiṁ gāyati bhakti⋅pūrvaṁ
prāpnoti suprema⋅sudhāṁ saḥ sarvam
tritāpa⋅dāvānala⋅duḥkha⋅muktaḥ
pramodate kṛṣṇapadābja⋅bhaktaḥ
en_translation:
He who has stolen the waves of the beauty and qualities of Śrīmatī Rādhikā, whose complexion is golden like molten gold, and who is the essence of the Vedānta, Vedāṅgas, and Purāṇas—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He whose lotus feet are praised by Brahmā, Indra, and Śiva, who is the abode of the ocean of magnanimity and sweetness, and who bears the burden of ecstasy manifested as horripilation, trembling, and tears—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who is the life and soul of Svarūpa Dāmodara and Rūpa Gosvāmī, who is the Lord of Gopāla, Govinda, and Mukunda, and who tears apart the misery, low birth, and sins of the poor—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who removes the darkness of the illusory philosophy of Māyā, who delivered the group of sannyāsīs in Vārāṇasī, and who spreads pure, transcendental devotion—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who removed the pride of the world-conquering brāhmaṇa, who bestowed great mercy upon Sārvabhauma Bhaṭṭācārya, and who enjoyed pastimes in Jagannātha Purī for eighteen years—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who bestows the mellows of brilliant love, who establishes the supreme devotion to the Holy Name, and whose eternal pastimes are in Goloka Vṛndāvana—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who is always intoxicated by the sweet singing of the Hare Kṛṣṇa mantra, who is the object of meditation for the best of yogīs and sages, and who gives the sweet essence of the love of Vraja—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He whose chest is broad like a door and whose eyes are like fresh lotus flowers, whose body is the condensed form of eternity, knowledge, and bliss, and whose bodily luster defeats millions of Cupids—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who is like a cakora bird for the nectar of the moon of Nīlācala, who is agitated by the nectar of singing in front of the Ratha-yātrā chariot, and whose body shines amidst the assembly of Vaiṣṇavas—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who is the royal swan in the minds of the devotees, who is the crown jewel of the sannyāsīs and brāhmaṇas, and who is the son of Jagannātha Miśra and Mother Śacī—may that Lord Gaura, the incarnation of mercy, be victorious.

He who sings this praise of Lord Gaura with devotion obtains the nectar of pure love. Freed from the misery of the forest fire of the three-fold sufferings, the devotee of the lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa rejoices.
